commands.review_summary:
The PR converts Gateway service doctor repair checks into structured health checks with read-only detection, repair notes, dry-run effects, and focused tests while preserving legacy wrapper entrypoints.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: mock detectGatewayServiceConfigIssues with serviceRewriteBlocked true plus a remaining issue, then call the structured repair path and observe it still returns update effects even though repairGatewayServiceConfig returns before service mutation.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer handling because the PR is protected/stacked, has concrete P2 repair-output bugs, and still needs contributor-provided real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: Doctor: structure gateway service repairs
target.body:
## Summary

Stacks on #83753, #84290, and #84326 as the next doctor repair conversion bucket.

This branch converts the gateway service repair checks to the structured doctor health-check path while preserving the old contribution positions:

- extra gateway-like service detection and legacy service cleanup
- gateway service config drift detection and repair
- gateway platform notes as structured read-only findings
- service-side effects represented as `effects[]` instead of fake config/file diffs

The converted checks now call structured gateway repair entrypoints (`repairExtraGatewayServices(...)`, `repairGatewayServiceConfig(...)`). The old `maybeScanExtraGatewayServices(...)` and `maybeRepairGatewayServiceConfig(...)` names remain as legacy contribution wrappers and delegate to the structured functions.

## Note / Finding Contract

This continues the note-phase contract from #83753:

- detect paths are read-only and silent
- `doctor --lint` receives findings only, not repair/operator notes
- `doctor --fix` uses `DoctorPrompter.note(message, title?)` for timing-sensitive repair presentation
- structured repair adapters route `prompter.note(...)` into ordered `repairNotes`
- normal legacy doctor prompters still render through `note(...)`

One important gateway-specific fix: service install-plan warnings are suppressed during detect/audit so `doctor` and `doctor --lint` do not print from detection, but the same warnings are emitted during actual repair when the install plan is built for mutation.

## Dry-Run / Diff / Effects

Gateway service repairs are process/service side effects, not renderable file/config patches from this layer. Dry-run therefore reports concrete `changes[]` plus `effects[]` such as:

- `would-remove-legacy-gateway-service` for legacy gateway cleanup
- `would-update-gateway-service-config` for service config drift
- `remove-legacy-gateway-service` / `update-gateway-service-config` after real repair

This keeps `--diff` honest: the converted checks do not invent file diffs for service-manager operations.

## Structured Repair Matrix

| Check | Detect / lint behavior | Repair behavior | Dry-run / effects | Note / validation proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Extra gateway services | Detects gateway-like/legacy services without printing. | Converted repair calls `repairExtraGatewayServices(...)`; legacy `maybeScanExtraGatewayServices(...)` delegates for old contribution paths. | `would-remove-legacy-gateway-service` and `remove-legacy-gateway-service` service effects. | Detect-after verifies removed legacy services are no longer reported in focused tests. |
| Gateway service config | Detects service config drift silently. | Converted repair calls `repairGatewayServiceConfig(...)`; legacy `maybeRepairGatewayServiceConfig(...)` delegates for old contribution paths. | `would-update-gateway-service-config` and `update-gateway-service-config` service effects. | Focused tests cover repair, dry-run, effects, and validation. |
| Gateway platform notes | Platform/service caveats are structured findings or repair notes, not detect-time prints. | Timing-sensitive repair presentation goes through shared `DoctorPrompter.note(...)`. | No synthetic config/file diffs for service-manager work. | Structured adapter routes notes into ordered `repairNotes`. |
| Install-plan warnings | Suppressed during detect/audit and lint. | Emitted during actual repair when the install plan is built for mutation. | Side-effect metadata stays explicit. | Tests cover silent detect/lint behavior and repair-time note routing. |

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway service doctor repairs now run through structured health checks at their original doctor contribution position, with silent detect/lint behavior, ordered repair presentation, detect-after validation, and concrete side-effect metadata.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on branch head `5e24059d6d36bc624804f9d8d5969ec2267ccd07` using focused Vitest coverage and formatting/whitespace checks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-prompter.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Focused test output showed all selected tests passing: 4 files / 94 tests. Formatting and whitespace checks passed.

Observed result after fix:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       94 passed (94)
```

What was not tested: This proof uses mocked service managers and command helpers, not a live launchd/systemd install. It proves the structured contract, note routing, dry-run/effects metadata, and detect-after behavior for this bucket; live service-manager proof should be added before landing if maintainers want real OS service mutation evidence for this stacked branch.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-prompter.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
